title: Full 344-test wp-bench rerun on the v1.2 gen model, vLLM bf16 shipping stack
purpose: The 0.4484 figure in output/packaging/gate1_bf16_baseline.json was reused from Phase 14's consolidation report (output/eval3/eval3_final_comparison.json), which itself copied output/sieve/optimal_k.json's full_arm.wp_bench value (a Phase 11 k-sweep baseline arm), not a fresh full-suite measurement taken for packaging. BENCH-01 gives the shipped pair a current, honest, full-344-test wp-bench number measured on the actual shipping stack.

served_model.quantization: none — bf16 safetensors; the gen model was NEVER quantized (only the judge got the Q8 GGUF treatment in Phase 15)

serving_stack.enable_thinking_mechanism: scripts/_wpbench_pth/usercustomize.py — PYTHONPATH-loaded monkeypatch threads extra_body.chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking=false through wp-bench's litellm.completion() call (wp-bench's config schema has no native field for this); defensive <think>...</think> strip also applied
serving_stack.note: This is the identical serve_30_70_vllm.sh + _run_wpbench() code path (scripts/run_eval_reasoning.py) that produced the 0.4484 figure via scripts/sieve_ksweep_run.py's k=full arm — only the container port differs (8020 here, matching config/wp-bench.yaml/dgx_toolbox.yaml's documented default, vs. 8021 in the sieve driver), which has no effect on generation quality.

warmup_gate.detail: Phase 15 LOCKED lesson: capture was gated on a real one-word completion request returning non-empty content, not on vLLM's /v1/models health response.
warmup_gate.result: PASS — non-empty response observed before wp-bench run started

sampling_config.note: temperature=0.0 (greedy/deterministic) is wp-bench's ModelConfig default and was NOT overridden — this matches the exact params used by the code path (_run_wpbench) that produced the 0.4484 comparison figure; no separately-recorded non-default sampling config exists for that figure, so the deterministic default is the correct comparable choice per plan instruction.

run_config.limit_note: Full suite, unlimited — not a --limit subset.

wall_clock.includes: vLLM container boot (~521s / ~8.7 min) + real-generation warm-up + full 344-test wp-bench run (~10 min) + container teardown
wall_clock.note: Much faster than the ~1h empirical estimate in the wp-finetune:run-evaluation skill's timing table; that table's ~1h figure is a general full-run estimate across historical runs and is not a contradiction — this run measured cleanly end-to-end with no retries or timeouts.

comparison.gate1_source_chain: gate1_bf16_baseline.json -> output/eval3/eval3_final_comparison.json (quality_measured_vllm_full_arm) -> output/sieve/optimal_k.json (full_arm.wp_bench) -> scripts/sieve_ksweep_run.py's k=full arm via scripts/run_eval_reasoning.py::_run_wpbench
comparison.same_stack_attestation: Confirmed same stack both sides: vLLM engine, bf16 dtype, identical model checkpoint (models/qwen3-30b-wp-30_70-reasoning-merged-v4), identical enable_thinking=false injection mechanism, identical sampling config (temp 0.0, max_tokens 2048, timeout 1800s, concurrency 4), identical wp-core-v1 344-test suite unlimited. No GGUF/llama.cpp path was used anywhere in this measurement.
comparison.verdict: REPRODUCES within noise. The fresh full-suite measurement (0.4365) sits 1.19pp below the 0.4484 Gate-1 reference figure, well inside the measured seed-noise floor (5.20pp) established by this project's own 3-seed variance work. No serving-stack anomaly, no GGUF/quantization path, no regression signal. The 0.4484 figure is confirmed as an honest, current, reproducible number for the shipped v1.2 gen model on its shipping stack; small numeric drift is attributable to normal run-to-run seed/sampling noise inherent to this benchmark, not to a stack or model change.
